name: ALTRIA GROUP, INC.
summary: Altria Group, Inc. is a leading U.S. tobacco company operating through subsidiaries including PM USA (cigarettes), Middleton (machine-made large cigars), USSTC (moist smokeless tobacco), Helix (oral nicotine pouches), and NJOY (e-vapor products). Key brands include Marlboro, Black & Mild, Copenhagen, Skoal, on!, and NJOY ACE. The company operates primarily in the United States and is transitioning toward smoke-free nicotine products while maintaining its core smokeable tobacco business.

products.name: Marlboro
products.description: Principal cigarette brand of PM USA, largest-selling cigarette brand in the U.S. for over 50 years.

products.name: Black & Mild
products.description: Principal machine-made large cigar brand manufactured and sold by Middleton.

products.name: Copenhagen
products.description: Premium moist smokeless tobacco (MST) brand sold by USSTC.

products.name: Skoal
products.description: Premium moist smokeless tobacco (MST) brand sold by USSTC.

products.name: Red Seal
products.description: Value moist smokeless tobacco brand sold by USSTC.

products.name: on!
products.description: Oral nicotine pouches manufactured and sold by Helix.

products.name: NJOY ACE
products.description: Principal e-vapor product of NJOY; subject to ITC exclusion and cease-and-desist orders.

products.name: Ploom HTS
products.description: Heated tobacco stick device to be supplied by JTIUH for U.S. commercialization via Horizon joint venture, pending FDA authorization.

products.name: Marlboro HTS consumables
products.description: Heated tobacco stick consumables to be manufactured by PM USA for U.S. commercialization via Horizon, pending FDA authorization.

partnerships.partner_name: JTI (US) Holding, Inc. / Japan Tobacco Inc.
partnerships.description: 75/25 joint venture (Horizon Innovations LLC) for U.S. marketing and commercialization of heated tobacco stick products.
Equity method investment in ABI, accounted for using a one-quarter lag.
Equity method investment in Cronos, accounted for using a one-quarter lag.
